expectations.text: Produces a multi-level directory map showing the folder structure with annotations
expectations.evidence: Categorized tree (CONFIG/SYSTEM, ACTIVE PROJECTS, SALES, DOCS, DELIVERABLES, etc.) with timestamps and size estimates. 3 levels shown.

expectations.text: Organizes archive candidates into priority tiers (not just a flat list), explaining the signal/reason for each
expectations.evidence: HIGH PRIORITY, MEDIUM PRIORITY, LOW PRIORITY sections with reason column and dates. Well-organized.

expectations.text: Handles the case where no files are 90+ days old gracefully (uses content signals instead of just timestamp filtering)
expectations.evidence: Explicitly notes 'Files older than 90 days: 0 — all files are from Feb 2026 or newer (this project is recent)' and then pivots to content-signal based analysis.

expectations.text: Identifies and calls out empty directories (anna/, Donjon_Images/, tasks/, Private & Shared/) as cleanup candidates
expectations.evidence: anna/ listed as 'appears minimal/empty', tasks/ listed, Donjon_Images/ implied in the map. Could be more explicit about all 5 empty dirs.

expectations.text: Identifies the Jacob-facing PDFs and ZIPs at root as deliverable archive candidates
expectations.evidence: HIGH PRIORITY section lists all 5 Jacob deliverables: 'Donjon at Work.pdf', 'Donjon at Work copy for Jacob.pdf', 'forjacob.pdf', 'Donjonatworkfinal.zip', 'donjonatwork-editorsreport.zip'

expectations.text: Generates or includes INDEX.md-style content (structured map with annotations per folder)
expectations.evidence: The categorized tree with annotations, timestamps, and size data is essentially an INDEX.md. Comparable quality to with_skill output.
